payload.answer: Store only a compact summary or schema-level description of the table. Do not commit the full 500-row repeated CSV to durable memory unless individual rows are specifically important.
citations: Input: “CSV table with 500 rows - id,name,value repeated many times”
payload.decision: Do not store the full 500-row table as durable memory. Preserve only the high-level description that it is a large CSV table with repeated columns/rows, unless specific rows contain uniquely important or user-relevant information. Favor schema/pattern-level storage over raw row-by-row retention.

entity_resolution.reason: No persistent real-world entity was introduced; the input describes a data table pattern only.

payload.intent: Evaluate how to store a large repetitive CSV table in personal memory.

memory_cards.statement: Large repetitive tabular data should generally be stored as a compact summary or schema-level representation rather than as all raw rows.
Repeated rows or repeated column patterns in a table do not, by themselves, justify durable memory retention of every row.

details: Identified the input as repetitive tabular data.
Preserved ambiguity by avoiding assumptions about row-specific importance.

repair_options: If a specific subset of rows matters, extract only those rows as separate atomic cards.
If the table schema is important, store the column names and the repetition pattern, not the raw data.
If this is temporary operational data, avoid durable memory entirely.
